judgment.summary: Rapidly growing software company with strong revenue and earnings growth, but high valuation multiples and thin operating margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 18.3%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 32.2% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 113.4% indicate strong top-line and bottom-line expansion.
Forward P/E of 74.61, while high, is significantly lower than trailing P/E of 558.53, suggesting future growth is being priced in.
Gross margin of 79.9% is very healthy, indicating efficient core operations.
Free cash flow of $936.73M TTM and a strong current ratio of 3.40 suggest good liquidity and operational cash generation.
Consensus is strong buy with a mean analyst target of $223.02, above the current price.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 558.53 and Price/Sales of 20.57 are extremely high, indicating a very expensive stock.
Operating margin of 0.8% and Profit margin of 3.7% are thin, raising concerns about profitability despite high revenue growth.
Return on equity of 3.9% and Return on assets of -0.2% are relatively weak.
Debt/Equity ratio of 32.22, while potentially manageable with strong cash flow, warrants attention.

news.paragraph: Datadog's peer Cloudflare reported mixed results, beating expectations but seeing a muted market reaction, while Wedbush maintained a bullish stance on Dynatrace with a $48 price target. These headlines provide context for Datadog's upcoming earnings report, suggesting that while the sector may be seeing some investor caution, analyst sentiment remains largely positive for some players. Traders should monitor how Datadog's report on August 6th is received in light of these recent industry developments.

headlines.title: Wedbush Remains Bullish On Dynatrace (DT) With $48 PT And Outperform Rating
Cloudflare (NET) Q1 Results Beat Expectations, But Market Reaction Tells A Mixed Story
